subject: Remove legacy Policy Types no longer used
commit_message:
Remove legacy Policy Types no longer used

These were loaded in Dublin as a stop gap solution as we built up
the self-serve control loop infrastructure over the last 3 releases.
CLAMP does not use these anymore, so cleaning them out of the repo.

Issue-ID: SDC-3229
Change-Id: I39e3a8b8146629f4b9dbb02abe7a0acf9166e716
Signed-off-by: Pamela Dragosh <pdragosh@research.att.com>

entries.message:

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

PM-mapper and PMSH may be used - looped Joe/Fiachra from ericsson to confirm

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> PM-mapper and PMSH may be used - looped Joe/Fiachra from ericsson to confirm

Fiachara and Joseph, can these policy types be moved into policy framework as a pre-loaded Policy Type? Or are they a part of sdc distribution and required to be in the TOSCA csar for your use case?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > PM-mapper and PMSH may be used - looped Joe/Fiachra from ericsson to confirm
> 
> Fiachara and Joseph, can these policy types be moved into policy framework as a pre-loaded Policy Type? Or are they a part of sdc distribution and required to be in the TOSCA csar for your use case?

Strictly speaking these types arent used today, due to an issue with the monitoring policy being too detailed for some platform components to handle in DCAE, that fix will be introduced next release.
However, I suspect they could be moved to pre-loading on the policy side, as long as clamp is capable of pulling them from there.

Adding James, to see if he can get someone to look into the new approach and if it would impact the Control loop distribution for us.

Patch Set 1:

> 
> Strictly speaking these types arent used today, due to an issue with the monitoring policy being too detailed for some platform components to handle in DCAE, that fix will be introduced next release.
> However, I suspect they could be moved to pre-loading on the policy side, as long as clamp is capable of pulling them from there.
> 
> Adding James, to see if he can get someone to look into the new approach and if it would impact the Control loop distribution for us.

Are these part of a use case? If so, then I would follow ONAP Integration team guidelines where the policy types and policies remain in use case codebase. We pre-load cdap because its part of Integration team testing. Thus, would be better then to remove them from SDC anyway.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

OKay to remove these legacy types; Pam will submit separate MR to preload required policy types
